channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Lo que ‘El diablo viste a la moda’ enseñó a sus estrellas sobre el poder
item.description: Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, Stanley Tucci y el director David Frankel hablan de ‘El diablo viste a la moda’, de la secuela y de quién es malvado en la vida real.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik and Thea Traff

media:content.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Swapped’ Review: It’s Freaky Friday in the Animal Kingdom
item.description: Michael B. Jordan voices a woodland creature who swaps species with a bird in this middling animated movie on Netflix.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: Los islandeses aman sus piscinas. Pero que no se enteren los turistas
item.description: Las piscinas y bañeras de hidromasaje son centros de vida social, una distinción cultural recientemente reconocida por la UNESCO. Algunos no están tan entusiasmados con la designación.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization

media:content.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times

item.title: Picture Prompt: Zero Gravity
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Today’s Spelling Bee Hints for May 1, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Violence Erupts in Australian Town After Arrest of Suspect in Girl’s Killing
item.description: Police in Alice Springs fired rubber bullets and tear gas to disperse an angry crowd demanding justice.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

media:content.media:credit: Rhett Hammerton/Australian Associated Press, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A police vehicle burning outside a hospital in Alice Springs, Australia, on Thursday.

item.title: Qué significa ‘pernicious’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 76 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Seth Meyers Gives Trump an Earful About Ear Jokes
item.description: The “Late Night” host expressed secondhand embarrassment after the president referred to the “beautiful ears” and “super hearing” of a NASA official.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Dudamel recibe una emotiva bienvenida en Nueva York
item.description: El director venezolano Gustavo Dudamel dominó el escenario de la gala anual de primavera de la Filarmónica de Nueva York.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Dolly Faibyshev
item.category: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: pernicious
item.description: This word has appeared in 74 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: What to Know About ‘Welcome to Country’ Ceremony Used by Indigenous Australians
item.description: The tradition, dating back thousands of years, is used by Indigenous Australians to greet visitors to their ancestral lands.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

media:content.media:description: Joy Murphy Wandin, an Indigenous elder, delivering the “Welcome to Country” before a rugby game in Melbourne last year.

item.title: How Indigenous Acknowledgments Became a Target in Australia
item.description: Recognition of Aboriginal Australians has become standard at most public events. Increasingly, the right is taking aim at the practice.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim

media:content.media:description: Pastor Ray Minniecon speaking at the Anzac Day service in Sydney on Saturday.

item.title: En la guerra en Irán, los avistamientos de guepardos son una rara esperanza
item.description: Los conservacionistas dicen haber registrado varios nuevos ejemplares adultos y cachorros de guepardos asiáticos, una subespecie en peligro crítico que solo se encuentra en Irán.
item.dc:creator: Sanam Mahoozi and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Una hembra de guepardo asiático llamada Dalbar en el Parque Pardisan de Teherán en 2017. La especie está bajo la amenaza constante de los cazadores, de los vehículos que circulan a gran velocidad y de los perros salvajes.

item.title: En Irán, se vive con dolor e impotencia bajo una fachada de tranquilidad
item.description: Tras meses de agitación, muchos intentan seguir adelante con sus vidas mientras luchan en silencio contra el dolor, el estrés económico y la pérdida de esperanza.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una emotiva despedida en la frontera de Irán con Turquía antes de que una familia iraní se dirigiera a Australia para trabajar, sin saber cuándo se reunirían con sus seres queridos en su país de origen.

item.title: Trump Is the One Without the Cards at the Poker Table
item.description: A.I. will drastically increase the power of small states and groups in conflict with the great powers.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Evan Hume for The New York Times

item.title: India Is Prototyping a Dangerous New Model of Web Censorship
item.description: The Modi administration’s systematic throttling of free speech sets a dangerous precedent for the world.
item.dc:creator: Arman Khan

item.title: Cuando el ruido de la comida se detuvo
item.description: Antes del auge de los medicamentos GLP-1, los expertos en obesidad no estudiaban ese ruido interior que obliga a las personas a comer. Ahora que el ruido de la comida está cediendo, quieren entenderlo.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata

item.title: Después de Mythos, nadie está a salvo de las amenazas de ciberseguridad
item.description: La fantasía de los hackers se hizo realidad cuando el nuevo modelo de IA de Anthropic demostró que podía detectar vulnerabilidades de software.
item.dc:creator: Brett J. Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: Evan Hume para The New York Times

item.title: 10 momentos inolvidables de ‘El diablo viste a la moda’
item.description: Recordamos los momentos más memorables que convierten la película original en una de las favoritas de los fans.
item.dc:creator: Maya Phillips

item.title: Quote of the Day: As South Korea Ages, A.I. Callers Become Best Friends for the Elderly
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, May 1, 2026.

item.title: In Bulgaria, a New Government Challenges an Old Puppet Master
item.description: Delyan Peevski was a focus of protests that led to elections in April. A new government now promises to challenge his hold on power.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.category: Movement for Rights and Freedoms (Bulgarian Political Party)

media:content.media:description: Delyan Peevski greeting supporters in Sofia, Bulgaria, in 2024. To his critics, Mr. Peevski has come to embody Bulgaria’s failure to establish the rule of law.

item.title: Lithuania Says It Broke Up Russian Sabotage and Murder Plots
item.description: The arrests of nine people are reminders of the threat Russia poses in Europe at a time when Washington has shifted focus to the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Michael Schwirtz and Tomas Dapkus

media:content.media:description: Ruslan Gabbasov, who Lithuania says was the target of a Russian murder conspiracy, in Brussels in 2023.

item.title: Editors’ Note: May 1, 2026
item.description: The following Editors’ Note and correction appeared in print on Friday, May 1, 2026.

item.title: Oil Companies’ Huge Profits Revive Calls for Temporary Windfall Taxes
item.description: European nations imposed temporary taxes in the 2022 energy shock when Russia invaded Ukraine, but whether they can effectively help households is up for debate.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An Exxon Mobil refinery in Texas. The United States has not been hit as hard by energy price increases as countries in Asia and Europe.

item.title: The Girl With the Sunglasses Tattoo
item.description: My daughter left me way too young, so I find ways to keep her with me.
item.dc:creator: Lindzi Scharf

item.title: A Wedding That Included a Mister and ‘The Miz’
item.description: Madison Greco and Steven Lynch bonded over their love of professional wrestling. The bride’s favorite wrestler led their wedding ceremony in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: Turning a House Into a Home — and a Wedding Venue, Too
item.description: Matthew Quinn and Blair Carnahan took on redesigning their new home in just four months. Then they took on another major project: planning their nuptials there.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: Two Brooklynites Find Love on Reddit
item.description: After connecting through an online post, Giuliana Cerullo and Abhipsit Srivastava met face-to-face and went on to build a relationship grounded in shared rituals that brought them closer.
item.dc:creator: Abby McCarter

item.title: By the End of a Hiking Trip, Their Relationship Was Elevated
item.description: Alyssa Levine and Liam Gallagher, both doctors, met through competitive swimming in 2010. A decade later, they felt sparks on Mount Shasta.
item.dc:creator: Louise Rafkin

item.title: Where in the World Is All That Gold Stored?
item.description: As central banks buy more gold, where to put all that heavy metal is an increasingly important question. Reserves must be secure and ready to trade in a crisis.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Horton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: More than 60 central banks store gold at the Bank of England.

item.title: Why Countries Are Stocking Up on Gold
item.description: As risk has escalated, central banks have bought more gold to stash in reserve. A widening Middle East war could add to the urgency.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

item.title: What’s Lost When We Give Up Driving
item.description: Will self-driving cars mark the end of a rite of passage for teens? On “Interesting Times,” the columnist Ross Douthat asks the author Andrew Miller whether the decline of manual driving signifies a broader shift toward the “safety focus and screenification of American youth.”
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: What if Your Waymo or Tesla Hallucinates?
item.description: Self-driving cars can still make mistakes, sometimes with frightening consequences. On “Interesting Times,” the author Andrew Miller joins the columnist Ross Douthat to discuss the inhuman errors these cars sometimes make, and whether they can be hacked.
item.dc:creator: Interesting Times

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 01, 2026
item.description: Rena Cohen opens our solving weekend with her themeless debut.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:description: Patience is the key to solving this Friday crossword.

item.title: ‘Moby Dick’ Review: Robert Wilson’s Last Masterpiece
item.description: Wilson’s 2024 adaptation of Herman Melville’s classic, with music by the British singer-songwriter Anna Calvi, has a short run at the Brooklyn Academy of Music.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: Christopher Nell, left, as the sublime Boy and Rosa Enskat as Captain Ahab in Robert Wilson’s “Moby Dick” at the Brooklyn Academy of Music.

item.title: El primer vuelo directo desde EE. UU. aterriza en Caracas después de 7 años
item.description: Funcionarios de American Airlines, del condado de Miami-Dade, del gobierno de EE. UU. y del gobierno venezolano celebraron el paso más reciente en el restablecimiento de los lazos entre ambos países.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager

media:content.media:description: El primer vuelo directo entre Estados Unidos y Venezuela aterrizó en Caracas el jueves por la tarde.

item.title: Schumer and Platner Talk After Mills Suspends Her Campaign
item.description: The top Senate Democrat and the Maine contender had what was described as a cordial conversation despite Schumer’s backing of his primary opponent.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse

media:content.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate in Maine’s Senate race, speaking at a town hall in Sabattus, Maine, on Wednesday.

item.title: California Police Can Start Ticketing Driverless Cars
item.description: The Department of Motor Vehicles says it could suspend or revoke permits for Waymo taxis and other driverless cars for continued violations.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Waymos parked in Los Angeles.

item.title: Pistachio-Cardamom Cake
item.description: This easy, buttery pistachio cake, topped with crunchy caramelized almonds, packs a punch of flavor, texture and perfume, especially from the cardamom seeds that permeate the batter.

media:content.media:credit: Linda Xiao for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini.

item.title: Musk vs. Altman: What Is This Really About?
item.description: In a landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI’s Sam Altman, the origins of OpenAI are being examined. The Times’s technology reporter Cade Metz explains what’s behind it all.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz, Melanie Bencosme, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster and Karl Mollohan

item.title: FEMA Is Reversing Job Cuts Made Under Kristi Noem
item.description: The agency said staff members who had been let go or placed on administrative leave were now needed to prepare for hurricanes and the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance

media:content.media:description: Former Homeland Security Kristi Noem during a visit to FEMA headquarters in Washington in January.

item.title: Chick-fil-A Bilked for $80,000 in Fake Mac-and-Cheese Refunds, Police Say
item.description: A former employee returned to a franchise in Grapevine, Texas, to process 800 phony refunds to his credit cards, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: A former employee returned to a Chick-fil-A franchise and used a mac-and-cheese menu item to snatch $80,000, according to the authorities.

item.title: Takeaways From Hegseth’s Second Day of Testimony on the Iran War
item.description: The secretary was also questioned over civilian deaths, an accusation of antisemitic remarks and women in combat.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, left, and Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, testifying before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Thursday.

item.title: N.Y. Archdiocese Offers $800 Million to Settle Sex Abuse Claims
item.description: Lawyers representing the abuse survivors warned their clients that the archdiocese would most likely file for bankruptcy if they did not agree to the settlement.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays and Maya King

media:content.media:description: The Archdiocese of New York sold some of its most valuable real estate holdings to fund sex-abuse settlements, including its headquarters on First Avenue in Manhattan.

item.title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
item.description: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The administration does not yet have the funding or congressional support for the weapons it needs for the possible future wars.

item.title: El dilema de Sheinbaum: desafiar a EE. UU. o arrestar a un aliado
item.description: El gobierno de Trump quiere que la presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum detenga a un gobernador mexicano. Ella cuenta con pocas buenas opciones como respuesta.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:description: La presidenta de México, Claudia Sheinbaum, podría detener a uno de los aliados de su partido, el gobernador del estado de Sinaloa, y extraditarlo a Estados Unidos, o desafiar la petición estadounidense y protegerlo.

item.title: Comey Indictment Shows Justice Dept. Got the Message From Bondi’s Firing
item.description: In naming only an interim successor as acting attorney general, President Trump has established even greater incentives to execute his most extreme demands, current and former officials say.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche, who is seeking to become the permanent attorney general, is currently examining a range of other potential charges against James B. Comey.

item.title: How to Give Yourself a Professional-Grade Face Massage at Home
item.description: A guide to getting a naturally sculpted look, using your hands, a gua sha tool or even a spoon.
item.dc:creator: Laura Regensdorf

item.title: Last on King Charles’s U.S. Tour: A Potluck and a Win for Scottish Whisky
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla had a ceremonial farewell at the White House before attending a block party in Front Royal, Va.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: The president and the first lady bidding farewell to King Charles III and Queen Camilla on Thursday.

item.title: Nigerian Military Runs a Fulani ‘Concentration Camp,’ Group Says
item.description: Amnesty International said about 1,500 people have been detained by the military for three months and that many of those who have died from disease and starvation have been children.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh and Ismail Auwal
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: Soldiers patrolling in a village in Kwara State, Nigeria, in February. The state has seen a surge in violent attacks in recent months.
